# Pi SearXNG Web Tools

Self-hosted search and readable-page extraction for the pi coding agent.

Repository: https://github.com/lenkard/pi-searxng-web-tools
Current release: v1.5.4

## Components

- `searxng`: patched SearXNG service on port 8888
- `api/app.py`: FastAPI search/fetch wrapper on port 8889
- `pi-extension/web-search-fetch.ts`: registers `web_search` and `web_fetch`
- `searxng/settings.yml`: committed engine-list source of truth; secrets stay in ignored `.env`

## API

- `GET /health`
- `GET /engines`
- `GET /engines/health`
- `GET|POST /websearch`
- `GET|POST /webfetch`
- Compatibility aliases: `/api/web_search` and `/api/web_fetch`

Search supports `fast`, `balanced`, and `deep` modes plus `engines=auto` intent routing. The safe sequential default is `bing,yep,mwmbl,wiby`. Google CSE remains available for focused searches.

General engines are health-checked every 15 minutes. Google-backed CSEs share one residential egress IP, so the monitor rotates one CSE at a time across two hours instead of issuing a burst. A CSE rate limit cools the shared group, and automatic routing falls back to the safe default chain.

## Install

```bash
cp .env.example .env
# set a generated SEARXNG_SECRET in .env
docker compose up -d --build
pi install git:github.com/lenkard/pi-searxng-web-tools@v1.5.4
# restart pi or run /reload
```

The pi package installs only the extension; Docker services must run separately. The extension defaults to the private Kinkaid WireGuard endpoint `http://172.25.0.7:8889`. Other deployments must set `PI_WEB_API_BASE_URL`.

Optional auth uses `WEB_API_KEY` on the API and `PI_WEB_API_KEY` in pi.

## Validation

Use only the paced routine benchmark:

```bash
python3 scripts/benchmark.py
```

Do not repeatedly bulk-test CSEs; their Google traffic shares one egress IP.

## Security

- Ports bind to localhost by default; use only a private bridge/VPN bind when remote access is needed.
- Do not expose ports 8888 or 8889 publicly without authentication and rate limiting.
- `web_fetch` rejects non-global IPv4/IPv6 destinations and checks every redirect hop.
- Never commit `.env`; `server.secret_key` comes from `SEARXNG_SECRET`.
